name: vitest-testing
description: **AI-friendly comprehensive testing guidance for Vitest with practical patterns and behavior-driven development.**
capabilities: Apply F.I.R.S.T principles for quality tests (Fast, Isolated, Repeatable, Self-Checking, Timely)
Implement black box testing through public APIs
Create mocks, stubs, spies, and fakes with Vitest
Test async code with promises, async/await, and fake timers
Handle error scenarios and edge cases in tests
author: AdamManuel-dev
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing markdown guidance and example TypeScript test code. All static findings are false positives: backtick patterns are markdown code block syntax, 'weak crypto' references are mock code with comments recommending bcrypt, and system reconnaissance patterns are Vitest test function names (describe, it, expect). No actual code execution, network access, or file system operations beyond reading own files.
valueStatement: Writing reliable unit tests is challenging without clear patterns. This skill provides AI-optimized Vitest guidance with F.I.R.S.T principles, AAA pattern structure, black box testing strategy, and comprehensive examples for mocks, async code, and error scenarios.
limitations: Does not execute tests or modify project files
Does not provide test coverage analysis
Does not integrate with CI/CD pipelines

useCases.title: Generate Tests
useCases.description: AI agents use this skill to write consistent, high-quality Vitest tests following established patterns

useCases.title: Learn Testing Patterns
useCases.description: Developers learn Vitest best practices including mocking strategies and error testing approaches

useCases.title: Migrate Test Suites
useCases.description: Teams transitioning from Jest find quick reference guides for syntax differences and migration

promptTemplates.title: Basic Test
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a Vitest unit test for [function/component name] following F.I.R.S.T principles and AAA pattern. Include happy path and error cases.

promptTemplates.title: Async Testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Write tests for async function [name] that handles promises and potential errors. Use appropriate async testing patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Mocking
promptTemplates.prompt: Create comprehensive mocks for [dependency] in Vitest. Include stubbed return values and verification of interactions.

promptTemplates.title: Error Cases
promptTemplates.prompt: Write error handling tests for [function] covering validation errors, edge cases, and failure scenarios using Vitest matchers.

bestPractices: Use mocks for all external dependencies to keep tests fast and isolated
Test only through public APIs (black box approach) to make tests refactoring-resistant
Structure every test with clear Arrange-Act-Assert sections and descriptive names
antiPatterns: Testing private methods directly breaks encapsulation and makes tests fragile
Making real database or network calls in tests creates slow, flaky, order-dependent tests
Skipping error scenario testing leaves critical failure modes uncovered

faq.question: How does this skill differ from official Vitest documentation?
faq.answer: This skill provides AI-optimized decision trees, patterns, and examples focused on test quality principles rather than API reference.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for Jest projects?
faq.answer: Most patterns apply to Jest. See the quick-reference/jest-to-vitest.md for syntax differences and migration guidance.

faq.question: Does this skill generate executable test files?
faq.answer: No, the skill provides guidance and examples. Users must implement tests in their own projects.

faq.question: What test coverage should I aim for?
faq.answer: Focus on behavior coverage through public APIs. Aim for meaningful coverage of business logic, not arbitrary percentage targets.

faq.question: How do I test React components?
faq.answer: See patterns/component-testing.md for React Testing Library integration patterns with Vitest.

faq.question: Can this skill help with integration tests?
faq.answer: Yes, see patterns/integration-testing.md for testing database connections, external APIs, and service interactions.
